description: Serving one request at a time so the accelerator idles between calls and throughput collapses while requests queue.
body: Failure mode: the server processes requests serially with no batching, so the GPU sits idle between calls and a backlog forms even though per-request compute is fast. Detection signals: GPU utilization low while a request queue grows; throughput far below the accelerator's batched capacity; latency rising from queue wait, not compute. Do not serve a batch-efficient accelerator one request at a time. Safer fallback: enable dynamic or continuous batching with a max size and queue timeout, then confirm utilization and throughput rise without breaching p99.
